---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0c-name-resolution
key: sv0c-name-resolution
state: done
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0c
    - name-resolution
    - scope
title: "sv0c name resolution"
type: task
---

# sv0c name resolution

## purpose

implement name resolution for sv0c in SML/NJ. this pass transforms an
untyped AST into a resolved AST where every identifier is bound to its
definition. it also builds the module dependency graph.

name resolution detects:
- undefined names (use of undeclared variables, types, functions)
- ambiguous imports (multiple definitions with same name)
- circular module dependencies
- visibility violations (accessing private items from outside module)
- duplicate definitions in the same scope

## dependencies

/require: sml

## configuration

/env: SV0C_ROOT=sv0c
/env: SRC_DIR=sv0c/sml

## implementation

### step 1: scope and environment

/ai: implement the scope/environment data structures in SML/NJ for sv0c.

     create src/name_resolution/resolver.sml with:

     - a scope type that maps names to definitions
     - nested scopes (function body creates new scope, block creates new scope)
     - module-level scopes for cross-file resolution
     - support for the module path resolution rules:
       1. crypto::ghash resolves to crypto/ghash.sv0
       2. crypto::ghash resolves to crypto/ghash/mod.sv0
     - import handling for all use forms:
       - use crypto::aes (import module)
       - use crypto::aes::encrypt (import item)
       - use crypto::aes::{encrypt, decrypt} (import multiple)
       - use crypto::aes::* (glob import)
       - pub use crypto::aes::encrypt (re-export)
     - visibility checking (private, pub, pub(crate))

### step 2: resolution pass

/ai: implement the name resolution pass that traverses the AST and resolves
     all identifiers. each identifier node in the resolved AST should carry
     a reference to its definition (or an error if undefined).

     handle:
     - variable bindings (let introduces a name into scope)
     - function parameters (create scope for function body)
     - struct/enum/trait/impl names (module-level scope)
     - type parameters in generics
     - pattern bindings in match arms
     - self and Self in impl blocks
     - shadow detection (warn, don't error)

/run: 01-build-resolver.sh

### step 3: resolver tests

/ai: write tests for name resolution covering:
     - simple variable lookup
     - nested scopes and shadowing
     - module imports (all use forms)
     - undefined name errors
     - duplicate definition errors
     - cross-module resolution
     - visibility violations

/run: 02-test-resolver.sh

## validation

/assert: test -f $SRC_DIR/name_resolution/resolver.sml
